data.title: EU Unveils Android AI Proposals Targeting Google's Ecosystem Control
data.description:
Pressure is building again around Alphabet Inc. , and this time the focus is narrowing on how AI is embedded across Android. European Union regulators have outlined a fresh set of proposals that could begin to reshape the competitive landscape, particularly around how third-party AI services access core system functionality. While the move stops short of a formal investigation, it may signal a more assertive phase of enforcement if the company does not move toward compliance.

At the center of the discussion, the European Commission is pushing for changes that would require Google to ensure competing AI services can effectively interact with Android applications and execute tasks on users' devices. The proposal directly addresses the advantage currently held by Gemini, which integrates across key Android features, including app communication and voice activation. Regulators are now seeking to extend that same level of access to rivals, a shift that could broaden user choice across AI services. EU competition chief Teresa Ribera indicated the measures are designed to expand options for users, while regulators are also gathering industry feedback before determining next steps.

Google has responded critically, arguing that the proposed intervention could increase costs while potentially undermining privacy and security protections for users. The measures fall under the EU's Digital Markets Act, which has already drawn criticism from Donald Trump for targeting American technology firms. So far, enforcement has remained relatively measured, with fines issued to Apple Inc. and Meta Platforms Inc. , but the risk of escalation remains. If Google does not align with the latest guidance, regulators could move toward a formal probe, potentially adding to the nearly 9.5 billion in fines the company has accumulated in past cases.

data.title: Chip Stocks Wipe Out as Iran War Fears and Big Tech Earnings Loom
data.description:
AI and chip stocks fell on Monday as investors awaited quarterly results from major technology firms and monitored U.S.-Iran war developments. Nvidia edged higher while the broader sector faced selling pressure.

The Nasdaq Composite dipped about 0.25%, the S&P 500 slipped 0.12% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.24%. Oil prices surged, with Brent crude up about 3% and U.S. crude nearly 3%, weighing on growth-sensitive tech shares.

Iran offered to lift its blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil transit chokepoint, if the U.S. ends its own blockade and the war, the Associated Press reported. The shipping disruption has created inflationary pressures, raising cost concerns for data-center hardware suppliers tied to Nvidia's ecosystem.

Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) fell nearly 4%, Marvell Technology tumbled nearly 5%, ARM slumped about 9% and Applied Optoelectronics plummeted about 10%. Nvidia shares rose about 1%, while Micron Technology (MU) jumped about 5% and Intel rose about 1%.

data.title: Big Tech Lobbying Push Defeats California Antitrust Bill In Weeks
data.description:
The swift collapse of California's Based Act is starting to look less like a routine legislative setback and more like a case study in how deeply entrenched Big Tech's influence could still be. Within weeks of its introduction by state Senator Scott Wiener, the bill designed to prevent platforms from favoring their own services over competitors ran into a coordinated resistance from industry leaders including Apple Inc. and Alphabet Inc. . Backed by smaller firms and groups such as Y Combinator, the proposal initially signaled a potential shift toward tighter oversight, but the momentum faded quickly as opposition intensified.

The response from the industry was both rapid and highly structured, echoing earlier federal-level battles. Trade groups like the California Chamber of Commerce and Chamber of Progress moved early to frame the bill as a risk to consumers, warning it could make search results less useful, deliveries slower, and devices less secure. At the same time, company executives stepped in directly an unusual move at this stage of a state proposal arguing the measure could resemble European-style regulations that might require significant compliance efforts and potentially divert resources away from product development. The strategy closely mirrored prior campaigns where major platforms collectively deployed substantial lobbying and advertising spend to block similar legislation.

The bill ultimately failed on April 20 after losing a key committee vote, reinforcing how policymakers continue to weigh the tech sector's economic importance alongside regulatory concerns. Lawmakers pointed to the industry's role in employment, tax revenue, and local economies, suggesting that any policy shift may need to balance those factors carefully. For investors, the outcome could indicate that near-term regulatory pressure in the US may remain limited, even as figures like Wiener signal potential follow-up efforts. While the broader debate around competition policy is unlikely to fade, the latest episode suggests Big Tech's defensive playbook remains effective at least for now.

data.title: Hut 8 Markets $3 Billion Bonds For AI Data Center Backed By Google Lease
data.description:
Hut 8 is moving deeper into the artificial intelligence infrastructure buildout, with a project marketing secured bonds due 2042 to help finance a data center tied to Alphabet Inc.'s Google . Initial price talk points to a yield of about 2.13 percentage points above the benchmark, according to a person with direct knowledge of the matter, as the company looks to tap into investor demand that has been building across AI-linked credit markets. The deal, being led by Goldman Sachs Group Inc., JPMorgan Chase & Co., and Morgan Stanley, is expected to raise at least $3 billion to support a 245-megawatt facility in St. Francisville, Louisiana.

The timing reflects a broader shift across the market, where data center developers are increasingly tapping multiple corners of the debt markets to fund the computing capacity required for AI's expansion. Wall Street has already secured tens of billions of dollars in financing in recent weeks, with investors continuing to absorb supply as demand for infrastructure tied to AI workloads could be accelerating. Hut 8's project appears structured to align with this trend, offering long-duration exposure to contracted revenue streams in a segment where visibility may be improving.

At the project level, the 600,000-square-foot facility is backed by a 15-year lease with Fluidstack Ltd., valued at about $7 billion, while Google is providing a financial backstop covering lease payments and other obligations. A subsidiary of Entergy Corp. will supply electricity under a services agreement. The financing also highlights Hut 8's ongoing transition from its origins as a Bitcoin miner into energy and digital infrastructure, a shift that could influence how investors assess its positioning across both crypto and AI-linked markets.

data.title: Meta pokes holes in China's great AI firewall
data.description:
By Karen Kwok

Beijing ordered Mark Zuckerberg to unwind the $2 bln acquisition of autonomous agent Manus, which had moved to Singapore. Belatedly enforcing foreign investment rules will be messy. It does send a clear warning that also exposes the trouble with global aims and local restraints.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Karen Kwok on LinkedIn and X.

CONTEXT NEWS

China on April 27 ordered social media giant Meta Platforms to unwind its December 29 acquisition of artificial intelligence startup Manus, which relocated to Singapore after it was started in Beijing.

The National Development and ​Reform Commission said it would "prohibit foreign investment in Manus in accordance with laws and regulations, and requires the parties involved to withdraw the acquisition transaction."

The state economic planning agency did not name Meta or other overseas investors in Manus.

"The transaction complied fully with applicable law," Meta said in a statement to Reuters. "We anticipate an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."

data.title: Starboard Builds Stake In Flowserve, Push For Changes - Bloomberg News
data.description: • STARBOARD BUILDS STAKE IN FLOWSERVE, PUSH FOR CHANGES - BLOOMBERG NEWS

data.title: Microsoft Loses Its OpenAI Exclusivity, Shares Slip
data.description:
Microsoft fell 0.20% intraday trading after reports that the company will no longer hold exclusive access to OpenAI's AI models and products, clearing the way for OpenAI to sell across rival cloud platforms including Amazon and Alphabet's Google . Alphabet shares rose 2.10% on the news while Amazon slipped 0.40%. Under the deal, Microsoft retains a license to OpenAI's intellectual property through 2032 and remains its primary cloud partner, but will no longer pay a revenue share for OpenAI products sold on its cloud. The revenue OpenAI must share with Microsoft through 2030 now carries a cap and is no longer tied to technology milestones, including whether OpenAI achieves artificial general intelligence.

The change follows months of rising tensions. The Financial Times reported last month that Microsoft was weighing legal action over OpenAI's $50 billion cloud deal with Amazon, which may have breached the exclusive arrangement. An internal OpenAI memo reported by CNBC described demand since launching on Amazon's cloud as "staggering," and acknowledged the Microsoft partnership had limited its enterprise reach.

Ending the exclusivity pact may also help Microsoft fight antitrust scrutiny in the US, UK, and Europe over whether its OpenAI tie-up gave it an unfair advantage in cloud and enterprise AI markets. For OpenAI, the commercial logic is straightforward. Gil Luria, analyst at D.A. Davidson, said AWS and Google Cloud enterprise customers had been constrained in their ability to integrate OpenAI products and will now be more likely to consider OpenAI alongside Anthropic, widening the competitive pressure on one of OpenAI's closest rivals in the enterprise market.

data.title: Nasdaq Edges Higher; Oil Up After Peace Talks Stall — WSJ
data.description:
Oil prices climbed and U.S. stocks wavered after President Trump said he wasn't sending U.S. envoys to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran.

The most actively traded Brent crude futures contract, which expires in May, rose about 3% to trade around $102 a barrel. Major U.S. stock indexes moved off their lows in afternoon trading, with Nasdaq and the S&P 500 on pace to build on their Friday records.

Investors are set to digest a slew of earnings reports this week, with a fifth of S&P 500 companies reporting. Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta are all set to release first-quarter results Wednesday, with Apple following on Thursday.

It will also be a busy week for central banks, with policy decisions due from the Federal Reserve, the European Central Bank, the Bank of Japan and the Bank of England. The Fed is expected to keep rates unchanged at 3.50-3.75% at its meeting, which wraps up Wednesday.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Liberty Star Uranium & Metals 10-K: $0 Revenue, $(0.02) EPS, $1.244M Net Loss
data.description:
Liberty Star Uranium & Metals reported zero revenue for the year ended January 31, 2026, and recorded a net loss of $1.244 million, or $(0.02) per share, compared with net income of $2.122 million in the prior year. The company’s loss from operations was $(1.088) million as exploration costs were expensed and operations remain pre-revenue. Management flagged substantial doubt about the company’s ability to continue as a going concern without additional financing.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 — the company has not generated any revenue during the periods reported.
  • Operating Income: $(1.088) million — loss from operations for the year ended January 31, 2026.
  • Net Income: $(1.244) million — net loss for the year ended January 31, 2026 (prior year net income $2.122 million).
  • Net Income Per Share: $(0.02) — net loss per share of common stock, basic, for the year ended January 31, 2026.

Business Highlights

  • Exploration Focus: Continued acquisition and exploration of mineral claims in Arizona’s Tombstone Mining District aimed at identifying commercial mineral resources.
  • No Revenue Yet: The company remains in an exploration stage; exploration expenditures are expensed as incurred, delaying any transition to commercial operations.
  • Financing-Driven Operations: Ongoing convertible note financings have enabled continued exploration activity and provided short-term cash runway, but additional funding is required to advance planned programs.
  • Going Concern Risk: Management reports substantial doubt about the ability to continue as a going concern without obtaining further financing to support planned exploration activities.

Original SEC Filing: LIBERTY STAR URANIUM & METALS CORP. [ LBSR ] - 10-K - Apr. 27, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Poet Technologies Shares Fall After Marvell Cancels Celestial AI Orders
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Poet Technologies shares slid after Marvell Technology cancelled purchase orders from Celestial AI.

The stock sank 48% to $7.93 on Monday. Shares are still up 25% this year.

The optical engine developer said Marvell cancelled all of Celestial AI's purchase orders after acquiring the company.

Marvell also indicated that Poet had violated confidentiality obligations when making disclosures of information related to the purchase order and shipping information, Poet said.

Poet said during an earnings call last June that it had initiated its first shipments to Celestial AI. Marvell acquired Celestial AI this February.

Poet said it is focused on its other partnerships, including a recently disclosed purchase order with another technology company for $5 million.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Wheat Futures Climb on Multiple Risk Factors - Daily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

  • Wheat for July delivery rose 2.3% to $6.30 3/4 a bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade on Monday, with war-related risk premium returning to grains while farmers attempt to start the growing season on a good foot despite the weather.
  • Corn for July delivery rose 1.3% to $4.69 1/2 a bushel.
  • Soybeans for July delivery rose 1.1% to $11.91 1/4 a bushel.

HIGHLIGHTS

Less Than Ideal: Weather was the primary driver for grain futures Monday, with rainfall seen in eastern Corn Belt states including Iowa, Missouri, and Illinois, while western states stay beset by drought conditions. "Large sections of the central and southern High Plains remain extremely dry, with a significantly elevated wildfire threat and worsening drought impacts on rangeland, pastures, and winter wheat," said the USDA in its daily weather report Monday. That means this afternoon's Crop Progress report will hold a lot of sway in how grain futures trade overnight.

Stuck in the Squeeze: The continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz also supported higher grain futures, with talks appearing to be stuck. More eyes were on grain futures amid reports of fertilizer supply troubles, said the Hightower Report in a note.

INSIGHT

Boosting the Surplus: The appetite for long grain positions among fund traders appears to have returned in some capacity, according to the CFTC in its latest Commitments of Traders report. The agency said fund traders added over 27,000 long contracts in corn through April 21, bringing that net long among funds to over 184,000 contracts. Soybeans added just over 10,000 long contracts, bringing that long position to nearly 193,000 contracts among funds.

Pulling Back: Export inspections of U.S. grains fell from the previous week, according to USDA data. In its latest weekly Grains Export Inspections report published Monday, the USDA said that for the week ended April 23, inspections of U.S. corn were 1.64 million metric tons, soybeans landed at 628,826 tons, and wheat was 365,156 tons. All three of these totals were below last week, and in the case of corn and wheat below this time last year.

AHEAD

-Smithfield Foods Inc. will release its first quarter 2026 earnings report at 4:30 a.m. ET Tuesday.

-Mondelez International will release its first quarter 2026 earnings report at 4:05 p.m. ET Tuesday.

-Bunge Global will release its first quarter 2026 earnings report at 6 a.m. ET Wednesday.

-The EIA will release its Weekly Petroleum Status Update report at 10:30 a.m. ET Wednesday.

-Pilgrims Pride Corp. will release its first quarter 2026 earnings report after the stock market closes on Wednesday.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

data.title: Musk Seeks $134 Billion In Trial Challenging OpenAI's Structure
data.description:
The legal battle between Elon Musk and Sam Altman is moving into a decisive phase this week, with a trial that could shape the future trajectory of OpenAI and its relationship with key backer Microsoft . The dispute stems from Musk's allegations that OpenAI shifted away from its original nonprofit mission toward a for-profit model that benefited leadership and investors, while OpenAI has pushed back, arguing the transition was necessary to secure funding for its long-term artificial general intelligence ambitions. Musk is seeking as much as $134 billion in damagesfunds he has said would go to OpenAI's charitable armand is also asking the court to remove Altman and President Greg Brockman, while potentially reversing the company's restructuring completed in October.

The stakes around the case could be significant, particularly as OpenAI navigates what appears to be a critical period in its growth cycle, including a potential public offering following its reported $852 billion valuation in March. A forced change in governance or leadership could introduce instability similar to the brief 2023 removal of Altman, an event that nearly disrupted the company's operations as employees and investors pushed for his return. At the same time, competitors could be watching closely, including Musk's own xAI, which he co-founded in 2023, as well as broader players in the rapidly evolving AI landscape. Microsoft, which holds a 27% stake after investing billions beginning in 2019, has defended its involvement, stating its capital was essential to advancing OpenAI's technology.

The three-week trial could also act as a window into OpenAI's internal decision-making, with testimony expected from both Musk and Altman, along with potential witnesses such as Satya Nadella and other current and former executives. While several of Musk's initial claims have been dismissed or dropped, the remaining issuesincluding breach of charitable trust and unjust enrichmentwill be evaluated by a jury before a final ruling from the judge. Even if Musk does not ultimately prevail, the proceedings could still surface internal communications and strategic details that may prove valuable in the broader AI race, particularly as competition continues to intensify.

data.title: CBOT soybeans notch one-month high on rising crude oil prices
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade most-active soybean futures hit a one-month high on a continuous chart on Monday, with back-month soyoil contracts notching lifetime highs as crude oil prices lifted the soy complex.

  • Oil prices climbed about 3% to a two-week high on Monday as peace talks between the U.S. and Iran stalled and shipments through the Strait of Hormuz remained limited, keeping global oil supplies tight.

  • Traders are looking ahead to a mid-May meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping with hopes that China will agree to additional purchases of U.S. soybeans and other agricultural goods.

  • Farmers around the world are facing the second surge in fertilizer prices in four years due to the U.S. and Israeli war on Iran.

  • The Middle East is a leading fertilizer production hub, and much of the global fertilizer trade typically passes through the Strait of Hormuz, which has seen traffic brought to a standstill by the conflict.

  • CBOT May soybeans (SK26) settled 13-1/2 cents higher to $11.77-1/4 per bushel.

  • CBOT July soyoil (BON26) ended 0.34 cent higher to 71.67 cents per pound. July soymeal (SMN26) rose $8.70 to settle at $327.80 per short ton.

data.title: Meta Is Trying to Harvest the Sun. Here's the Plan. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Al Root

AI data centers need a lot of power. The AI industry is wrestling with solutions to that problem.

SpaceX, for instance, is looking to bring data centers to orbit, leveraging the power of the sun. Meta Platforms has another idea for using the giant fusion ball in the sky, which accounts for 99%-plus of all the mass in our solar system.

Monday, Overview Energy and Meta announced a first-of-its-kind agreement to bring space solar energy to data centers.

"This is the first of many of these deals," says Overview CEO Marc Berte. "The sky's the limit on what we can do with this [technology]."

Berte, an MIT-educated nuclear and aerospace engineer, founded Overview in 2022 with the goal of producing energy at scale.

The agreement gives Meta early access to a planned gigawatt of generating capacity. (That's enough to power 500,000 to 1 million homes.) Overview declined to provide financial details of the agreement.

But doesn't all solar power come from space? Well, yes, but Overview's satellites essentially gather sunlight in geosynchronous orbit and beam it down as near-infrared light to existing solar projects.

The general idea is to turn solar power projects into near-24/7 operations. Existing solar producers don't need significant upgrades, and near-infrared light is invisible to humans. The company plans to be in orbit by 2028 with operations in 2030.

The technology is fascinating. There are other options for space solar, with solar panels in space beaming microwaves to a receiver.

Cost, of course, is an issue. Reaching space is still relatively expensive, even though SpaceX has cut costs to reach orbit by more than 90% compared with the Space Shuttle. Still, data centers are looking for ways to power themselves without causing a consumer burden. Average U.S. electricity prices are up about 15% since ChatGPT was launched in November 2022. (They are up about 40% since before the pandemic.)

Meta stock was up 0.4% in midday trading on Monday, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average were flat and down 0.1%, respectively.

Gains left Meta stock up 3% year to date and up 24% over the past 12 months.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Weinstein's Saba Capital to raise new fund, sources say
data.description:
By Svea Herbst-Bayliss

Boaz Weinstein's Saba Capital plans to expand its investment activities in public and private business development companies, a type of closed-end fund that invests in small to mid-sized private companies in distressed businesses, with a new investment vehicle, sources familiar with the plans told Reuters.

The news comes after Saba recently completed tender offers for Starwood Real Estate Trust and Blue Owl Capital (Blue Owl II), acquiring $10 million in aggregate face value across 190 separate trades, the sources said. Substantially all came from the Starwood REIT, the sources added.

The sources said demand among wealth advisors whose clients are stuck in private BDCs and interval funds and want liquidity helped fuel Weinstein's decision to raise the new vehicle.

Saba alongside Cox Capital Partners offered to buy up to 6.9% shares in Blue Owl Corporation II at a roughly 35% discount to net asset value.

Weinstein, a prominent investor who won a bet against a JPMorgan Chase trader known as the London Whale has argued that private credit is facing significant stress and that investors are looking for a way to exit.

Sources close to Saba acknowledge that the aggregate volume of tenders was below initial expectations but that Saba still views the results as a meaningful validation of its thesis.

data.title: Ackman's Pershing Square IPO Targets $5B With 85% Institutional Demand
data.description:
Billionaire Bill Ackman (Trades, Portfolio) appears to be closing in on a more measured version of his latest public market push, with the IPO of Pershing Square USA Ltd. and Pershing Square Inc. expected to raise about $5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter. That figure would land at the low end of the originally targeted range, which had reached as high as $10 billion, while demand still looks constructive, with roughly 85% of the deal covered by institutional investors. The offering is expected to stop taking orders at 4 p.m. New York time on Monday and price on April 28 as scheduled, although final terms could still change as deliberations remain ongoing.

The structure of the deal points to a broader shift in how Ackman is positioning his platform, leaning further into permanent capital that could allow him to hold investments without the risk of redemptions. The $5 billion total includes a previously disclosed $2.8 billion private placement that depends on reaching a minimum raise threshold, and investors are being offered additional alignment through equity incentives. For every five shares of Pershing Square USA purchased, investors receive one share of Pershing Square Inc. at no additional cost, while private placement investors receive 1.5 shares for every five acquired. The closed-end fund structure means shares wont be continuously created or redeemed, which could result in trading at a premium or discount to underlying holdings over time.

The IPO also comes as Pershing Square continues to evolve its portfolio and capital base, with roughly $30.7 billion in assets under management and $20.7 billion in fee-paying assets as of the end of 2025, according to filings. Its London-listed vehicle, Pershing Square Holdings Ltd., has recently added positions in Meta Platforms Inc. , Amazon.com Inc. , and Hertz Global Holdings Inc. , reflecting ongoing deployment into large-cap and cyclical opportunities. The transaction, led by banks including Citigroup Inc. , UBS Group AG , and Bank of America Corp. , could mark a key step in scaling that strategy, even as the final size suggests investor appetite may be more calibrated than earlier expectations implied.

data.title: Trump Calls on Disney to Fire Jimmy Kimmel After Melania Joke — WSJ
data.description:
By Isabella Simonetti and Meridith McGraw

President Trump is calling on Disney's ABC to remove late-night host Jimmy Kimmel over a comedy bit last week that referred to first lady Melania Trump as an "expectant widow."

During a mock White House Correspondents' Dinner speech, Kimmel made a joke about Melania Trump, saying, "Mrs. Trump, you have a glow like an expectant widow." Kimmel, the host of "Jimmy Kimmel Live!" said on April 23.

"I appreciate that so many people are incensed by Kimmel's despicable call to violence, and normally would not be responsive to anything that he said but, this is something far beyond the pale," Trump said. "Jimmy Kimmel should be immediately fired by Disney and ABC."

Disney didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

Kimmel's comment came before Saturday, when suspected gunman 31-year-old Cole Allen opened fire outside the correspondents' dinner. Allen is being charged with using a firearm during a crime of violence, and assault on federal officers using a dangerous weapon.

The first lady sat next to the president when the shooting occurred and ducked under a table before she was ushered by security off stage. Trump later said the event was a "traumatic experience" for the first lady.

On Monday, Melania Trump called on Disney's ABC to "take a stand" against Kimmel:

"Kimmel's hateful and violent rhetoric is intended to divide our country. His monologue about my family isn't comedy- his words are corrosive and deepens the political sickness within America," she wrote on X. "How many times will ABC's leadership enable Kimmel's atrocious behavior at the expense of our community."

Write to Isabella Simonetti at isabella.simonetti@wsj.com and Meridith McGraw at Meridith.McGraw@WSJ.com

data.title: Rogers Communications offering buyouts to half its workforce, Globe and Mail reports
data.description:
Canadian telecom operator Rogers Communications is offering voluntary departure packages to half of its 25,000 employees, the Globe and Mail reported on Monday.

Here are some details:

  • Rogers on Monday said that employees across numerous business divisions will be offered packages, but did not say whether it had a reduction target, according to the report.

  • "We are taking steps to adjust our cost structure to reflect the business realities of the current environment. As part of this, some teams have chosen to offer voluntary departure and retirement programs to give some employees the choice to decide whether they'd like to stay with the company or begin a new chapter," Rogers spokesperson Zac Carreiro told the Globe and Mail.

  • Some teams across the company including on-air talent, Sportsnet employees at Rogers Sports and Media and union employees are not eligible, the report said.

  • Rogers did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

  • Separately, Microsoft is planning its first voluntary employee buyout in the Windows maker's 51-year history for a small percentage of its U.S. employees.

data.title: US natural gas futures edge up 1% as output drops, LNG exports surge
data.description:
Key points:
  • US gas output drops as low prices prompt production cuts by firms like EQT
  • Mild weather boosts storage levels to 8% above normal, LSEG and analysts report
  • LNG exports near record highs, supporting overall demand despite weak spot prices

By Scott DiSavino

U.S. natural gas futures edged up about 1% on Monday from a 17-month low in the prior session, boosted by a drop in output in recent weeks, lifted demand forecasts for this week and near-record liquefied natural gas exports.

On its second-to-last day as the front-month contract, gas futures for May delivery on the New York Mercantile Exchange rose 2.7 cents, or 1.1%, to settle at $2.55 per million British thermal units (mmBtu). On Friday, the contract notched its lowest close since October 29, 2024.

Futures for June (NGM26), soon to be the front-month contract, were up about 2% to $2.74 per mmBtu.

In the cash market, some power and gas prices in Texas (NG-WAH-WTX-SNL) and California (W-SP15-IDX) traded in negative territory for a third straight week as mild weather limited heating and cooling demand, which was met by ample amounts of hydro and other renewable sources of energy.

SUPPLY AND DEMAND

Financial firm LSEG said average gas output in the U.S. Lower 48 states has eased to 110.1 billion cubic feet per day (bcfd) so far in April, down from 110.4 bcfd in March. That figure compares with a monthly record high of 110.7 bcfd in December 2025.

Daily output was on track to drop by around 0.7 bcfd over the past five days to a preliminary 108.8 bcfd on Monday as low spot prices prompted energy firms like EQT , the second-largest U.S. gas producer, to cut production. Preliminary data, however, is often revised later in the day.

Analysts said mostly mild weather this spring has allowed energy firms to inject more gas into storage than usual, boosting inventories to a forecast 8% above normal levels during the week ended April 24 from 7% above normal during the week ended April 17.

Looking ahead, meteorologists forecast the weather will remain mostly near normal through May 12.

LSEG projected average gas demand in the Lower 48 states, including exports, would slide from 102.0 bcfd this week to 100.1 bcfd next week. The forecast for this week was higher than LSEG's outlook on Friday.

Average gas flows to the nine big U.S. LNG export plants have risen to 18.9 bcfd so far in April, up from 18.6 bcfd in March. That reading compares with a monthly record high of 18.7 bcfd in February.

data.title: SEI Investments 10-Q: Revenues $622.2M, EPS $1.40
data.description:
SEI Investments reported quarterly revenues of $622.2 million and diluted earnings per share of $1.40, driven by higher assets under management/administration and contributions from the Stratos acquisition. Net income attributable to the company was $174.5 million, reflecting operating strength and rising fee-based revenues. Management cited increased platform adoption and continued conversion toward separately managed accounts and strategist programs as operational drivers.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $622.2 million, up 13% year-over-year (noting Stratos contribution of $19.0 million).
  • Gross Profit / Income from operations: $189.486 million, up 21% year-over-year.
  • Operating Income: $189.486 million, up 21% year-over-year (includes corporate overhead of $36.1 million).
  • Net income: $176.787 million, up 17% year-over-year; net income attributable to SEI Investments was $174.487 million.
  • Diluted earnings per common share: $1.40, up 20% year-over-year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers and client activity: Growth driven by higher AUM/AUA and new client wins; strategic acquisition activity expanded advisor relationships and assets.
  • Channel and product mix: Continued migration from SEI fund programs into separately managed accounts and Strategist programs, increasing fee-based revenue mix.
  • Stratos acquisition: Added revenue contribution of $19.0 million and expanded the advisor network supporting approximately $39.9 billion in assets.
  • Platform adoption: SWP client conversions and growth increased information processing fees and platform-only assets (platform-only assets rose 31%).
  • Operational investments: Increased staffing, consulting and vendor costs to support growth; capitalized software projects (including SWP and SEI Scope) were placed into service to support platform capabilities.

Original SEC Filing: SEI INVESTMENTS CO [ SEIC ] - 10-Q - Apr. 27, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Montfort Capital Corp. Announces Resignations from Board of Directors
data.description:
TORONTO, April 27, 2026 /CNW/ - Montfort Capital Corp. ("Montfort" or the "Company") , announces that Janice Lederman and Paul Geyer have resigned from the Company's Board of Directors, effective April 26, 2026.

"We thank both Jan and Paul for their years of service as Montfort directors and committee members," said Howard Atkinson, Chair of the Board of Directors. "Their contributions to the Company have been meaningful."

The Company does not intend to appoint replacement directors at this time, as the Board's current composition is appropriate for the Company's scale of operations.

Further, Cortland Credit Lending Corporation ("Cortland") issued demands under certain credit facilities on Pivot Financial I Limited Partnership ("Pivot"), as direct obligor, and also upon the guarantors of Pivot's obligations to Cortland, including Montfort and Langhaus Financial Corporation. In response, the Company halted trading of its securities on Friday, April 24, 2026. Discussions over the weekend between the parties resulted in the retraction of the demands as the parties work towards an alternative solution. The Company will provide updates to its shareholders in due course.

About Montfort Capital Corp.

Montfort builds and manages private credit portfolios that have focused investing strategies for the institutional and accredited investors markets. For further information, please visit www.montfortcapital.com.

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release.

Forward-Looking Statements

This news release contains certain "forward-looking statements" and "forward-looking information" within the meaning of applicable Canadian securities legislation. Forward-looking statements and information are frequently identified by words such as "will," "expects," "intends," "anticipates," "plans," "believes," "estimates," "may," "could," "would, " "continue," or similar expressions, or by statements that certain actions, events or results may, will or are expected to occur. Forward-looking statements in this news release include, but are not limited to, statements regarding: the parties working towards a resolution of the matters relating to the credit facilities; the Company's intention to provide updates to shareholders in due course; and the appropriateness of the Board's current composition for the Company's scale of operations.

Forward-looking statements are based on assumptions and expectations that management believes to be reasonable at the time such statements are made, but are subject to known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors that could cause actual results, performance or achievements to differ materially from those expressed or implied by such statements. These factors include, but are not limited to: the possibility that Cortland may reissue demands under the credit facilities; the inability of the parties to reach a mutually acceptable resolution; the financial impact on the Company of its guarantee obligations; changes in market and economic conditions; and other risks described in the Company's continuous disclosure filings available under the Company's profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements. The Company does not undertake to update any forward-looking statements, except as required by applicable securities laws.

SOURCE Montfort Capital Corp.

/CONTACT:

Copyright CNW Group 2026

data.title: SocGen Weighs 9 Billion Risk Transfer Deal Covering 8% Portfolio
data.description:
Societe Generale (SCGLY) is weighing a sizable synthetic risk transfer tied to more than 9 billion of corporate loans, a move that could place it among a relatively small group of banks executing transactions at this scale. The potential deal, spanning exposures across markets including France and the US, is expected to cover roughly 8% of the reference portfolio, according to people familiar with the matter, though discussions remain ongoing and terms under its Junon SRT program could still shift as investor talks progress.

The backdrop here matters. Europe's SRT market has stayed active even as macro conditions have turned more volatile, with energy price swings linked to disruptions in oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz during tensions between the US and Iran. Activity has continued regardless. BNP Paribas has completed two SRT deals tied to 5 billion of loans in recent months, while Deutsche Bank has executed a $6.4 billion transaction. At the same time, Toronto-Dominion Bank and Banco Santander are also evaluating potential deals, suggesting investor appetite for these structures could still be holding up.

If completed, Societe Generale's transaction would follow a string of large benchmark deals that point to a broader trend taking shape. Austria's Erste Group finalized an SRT exceeding 10 billion to help finance its acquisition of Santander Bank Polska, while BNP Paribas previously priced a 10.5 billion deal linked to corporate loans. Banks typically use SRTs to transfer between 5% and 15% of loan risk, which could help support solvency ratios, manage exposure limits, and free up capital for potentially higher-return lending opportunities.

data.title: There's a pizza price war going on, and Domino's is feeling the pain
data.description:
By Bill Peters

Domino's will lean into 'pizza innovation' as one way to engage customers who are getting more selective with their spending

Domino's rivals Papa John's and Pizza Hut earlier this year said they would close hundreds of locations.

Domino's Pizza's growth prospects are buckling as the chain deals with a deep discount war in the pizza industry and more cautious customers - trends it said could lead to more store closures, at least for its rivals.

The company's softer-than-expected earnings results and forecast for the year arrived Monday as big restaurant chains try to cut prices or otherwise run promotional deals in order to regain consumers who have pulled back their spending after years of increases to menu prices and the cost of living.

They also arrived after rivals Papa John's (PZZA) and Yum Brands' (YUM) Pizza Hut said earlier this year that they would close hundreds of locations amid the weaker spending backdrop.

Shares of Domino's (DPZ) were down 9.3% on Monday afternoon, pacing the S&P 500's SPX decliners even after recovering a bit off their session lows. The stock is down 32.6% over the past 12 months.

"Consumer sentiment hit COVID-level lows, and ongoing inflation continued to impact purchase decisions," Domino's CEO Russell Weiner said during Domino's earnings call on Monday.

He said competition increased through the first quarter, as Domino's big national rivals offered deals that were "comparable if not identical." However, he argued that Domino's had the profits and the advertising budget to outlast competitors that are leaning harder on discounting and, in turn, putting more pressure on their chains' franchisees.

"Over time, we expect this pressure to contribute to more store closures on top of the roughly 450 closures our two public pizza competitors have already announced for 2026," he added.

Investors will get more detail on how consumers are feeling when Starbucks (SBUX) and Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) report results this week. Chipotle's outlook in February suggested caution on the year ahead. However, when Starbucks last reported results in January, executives said there were signs its turnaround efforts were taking hold.

Domino's on Monday said it now expects global retail sales growth to be up "mid-single-digits" for the year, compared with a forecast in February for a gain of around 6%.

Management also appeared to soften its forecast in the U.S. The company said it expects U.S. same-store sales to be up "low-single-digits" for the year, following prior expectations for a 3% gain. Weiner on Monday said a 3% gain in U.S. same-store sales was still the company's "objective."

Still, for its first quarter, Domino's said it increased its order count as well as its market share in the U.S. Revenue came in at $1.15 billion, up a bit from $1.11 billion in the prior-year quarter but below FactSet estimates for $1.16 billion. U.S. same-store sales were up 0.9%, below estimates for a 2.3% increase.

Domino's reported adjusted earnings per share of $4.13, below $4.33 in the prior-year quarter as well as below Wall Street's estimates for $4.27.

Following those results, Weiner said during Monday's call that Domino's would try to bring more new items to its menu to revive consumer enthusiasm.

"It's not just value," he said. "I think we can do a little bit more on pizza innovation as well. And so, starting as soon as May, you're going to see things on the calendar or in media from Domino's that weren't on our calendar to start the year."

-Bill Peters

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Focus Universal Buys $17.7M Monterey Park Office; Secures $11.05M East West Bank Loan
data.description:
Focus Universal acquired a 100,743-square-foot Class A office and commercial property in Monterey Park, California, for $17.7 million and financed the purchase with an $11.05 million term loan from East West Bank. The asset is 99.2% leased to approximately 16 tenants, features a four-level parking structure with solar panels under lease, and the company intends to occupy about 2,000 square feet. The loan carries a 6.25% fixed rate for three years, then floats at WSJ Prime plus 0.25% with a 6.25% floor, and matures in 2036. Lee & Associates will manage the property during the transition.

Agreement 1: Focus Universal Buys Monterey Park Office Property for $17.7 Million; 99% Leased

  • Agreement type: Purchase and Sale Agreement for Class A office and commercial building
  • Counterparty: 901 Corporate Center
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 21 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Reason: Expand real estate footprint and rental income

Agreement 2: Focus Universal Secures $11.05 Million Term Loan With East West Bank at 6.25%

  • Agreement type: Secured real estate term loan
  • Counterparty: East West Bank
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 30 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 10 years
  • Reason: Finance the Monterey Park property purchase

Original SEC Filing: FOCUS UNIVERSAL INC. [ FCUV ] - 8-K - Apr. 27, 2026
